title: Failure Classification, Retry Budgets, Backoff, and Load Shedding · TypeScript Build Room
summary: A messaging pairing room predicts publisher, exchange, queue, consumer, ownership, and failure transitions before assembling the first failure taxonomy and bounded retry policy for failure classification, retry budgets, backoff, and load shedding. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of failure taxonomy and bounded retry policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence d…
objectives: Classify malformed, unauthorized, missing dependency, rate limit, timeout, conflict, transient, permanent, and ambiguous effect failures by evidence.
Bound attempts, total age, delay, processing deadline, queue residence, and operator escalation as one retry budget.
Apply exponential backoff and jitter without blocking consumer slots or requeueing hot loops and preserve intended availability time.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Transient Permanent Ambiguous
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop messaging team handles case 3554 in a partner publisher using an unroutable changed topic key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to classify permanent and ambiguous outcomes before applying both attempt and age budgets; prove topology rollback before deleting the old binding; then lower the byte or in-flight budget and preserve upstream rejection evidence. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?
